questions.explanation: git checkout -b <branch> creates the branch and checks it out in one step.
questions.question: In Git, which command creates a new local branch named "feature/login" and switches to it?

questions.explanation: Dedicated test attributes (e.g., data-testid) are designed to be stable and less affected by layout or copy changes.

options.B: CSS selector using a dedicated data-testid attribute
options.D: Link text that matches visible UI copy exactly

questions.question: Which locator strategy is generally the most stable for UI automation when the application provides unique attributes intended for testing?

questions.explanation: Selenium automates browsers to drive and validate web UI behavior.

options.A: A tool for compiling Java projects and managing dependencies
options.B: A browser automation library for automating web UI interactions
options.C: A test reporting dashboard for aggregating results
options.D: A CI server for running pipelines

questions.question: Which statement best describes what Selenium is used for?

questions.explanation: PUT is typically used to replace/update a resource at a known URI.
questions.question: In REST APIs, which HTTP method is most commonly used to update an existing resource (full replacement)?

questions.explanation: Surefire supports selecting a class and method with -Dtest=ClassName#methodName.
questions.question: You need to run a specific JUnit 5 test method named "shouldCreateUser" from the command line using Maven. Which Maven command is appropriate?

questions.explanation: Page Objects should expose behaviors and state accessors; assertions are usually kept in tests to avoid coupling pages to test expectations.

options.A: Inside the Page Object methods, mixed with click/type actions
options.B: In the test layer (or a dedicated assertion/helper layer) that uses Page Object methods
options.C: In the browser driver configuration code

questions.question: In the Page Object Model, where should assertions (e.g., verifying a success message text) typically live to keep the design maintainable?
questions.topic: Test Design & Automation Patterns (Page Object Model)

questions.explanation: A matrix strategy runs the same job with different parameters (e.g., browsers) in parallel on PR events.

options.A: Run tests locally and attach screenshots to the PR manually
options.B: Use a GitHub Actions workflow with a matrix strategy for browsers and enable parallel jobs
options.C: Commit the node_modules directory so CI runs faster
options.D: Run UI tests only on the main branch after merge to avoid CI usage

questions.question: A team wants every pull request to run UI tests in parallel across Chromium and Firefox using GitHub Actions. Which approach best fits this requirement?

questions.explanation: Condition-based explicit waits synchronize with real UI readiness (visibility/enabled/clickable) and reduce flakiness better than sleeps or blanket workarounds.

options.A: Add a fixed sleep (e.g., 5 seconds) before every click
options.B: Use an explicit wait for a condition like "element is visible and enabled/clickable" before interacting
options.C: Increase the global implicit wait to 60 seconds and remove all explicit waits
options.D: Click using JavaScript execution for every element to bypass the UI state

questions.question: Your UI tests are flaky because elements are sometimes present in the DOM but not yet clickable due to animations and overlays. Which solution is the most robust across Selenium/Playwright-style tools?

questions.explanation: A reusable client/service layer centralizes cross-cutting concerns (base URL, headers, retries, logging) and keeps tests focused on assertions and scenarios.

options.A: Copy-paste the HTTP request code into each test for clarity
options.B: Create a reusable API client/service layer (wrapper) that centralizes request building and exposes domain-specific methods
options.C: Store all requests as raw curl commands in comments and execute them manually when needed
options.D: Put all endpoint calls into a single test method and branch with if/else for different scenarios

questions.question: You are designing automation components for API tests. Multiple tests need to call the same REST endpoints with consistent headers, base URL, retries, and logging, while keeping test cases readable. What is the best design approach?

questions.explanation: Allure needs result files (e.g., allure-results) produced by the adapter and then collected/published; misconfigured output paths or missing artifacts commonly leads to empty reports.

options.A: The CI runner does not have a graphical browser installed
options.B: Allure results files were not generated or not collected from the correct output directory as pipeline artifacts
options.C: Git history is shallow (depth=1) so reports cannot be created
options.D: The tests used too many assertions, causing Allure to skip them

questions.question: Your CI pipeline publishes an Allure report, but the report shows '0 tests' even though the test runner logs indicate tests executed. Which is the most likely root cause?
